concept: Fresh / Recently Made / Vital and New
description: Recently made, produced, or harvested; not stale, spoiled, or faded. 'Newai' names the quality of recent vitality—bread just baked, air after rain, energy not yet depleted.
sound_symbolism: The word wakes to newness. 'Ne' begins with gentle opening, something new. Then 'wa' flows with waking life. Finally 'i' rises with vital energy, freshness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'newai purekoi' (fresh fruit). Related to 'newu' (new) but emphasizes vitality and recent origin rather than novelty.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fresh food, fresh water—traditional life depended on knowing freshness, preserving what could be preserved, using what must be used.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Fresh local food reduces transport and storage—freshness as both quality and sustainable practice.
